24	def test_n_gt_1(model):
25	    """ParallelSampling is supported."""
26	
27	    params = SamplingParams(n=3)
28	    outputs = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
29	    assert len(outputs[0].outputs) == 3
30	
31	
32	def test_best_of(model):
33	    """Raise a ValueError since best_of is deprecated."""
34	
35	    params = SamplingParams(n=2, best_of=3)
36	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
37	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
38	
39	
40	def test_penalties(model):
41	    """Check that we do not get errors if applied."""
42	
43	    params = SamplingParams(
44	        temperature=1.2,
45	        presence_penalty=1.2,
46	        frequency_penalty=1.2,
47	        repetition_penalty=1.2,
48	        min_p=0.5,
49	        top_p=0.5,
50	        top_k=3,
51	    )
52	    _ = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
53	
54	
55	def test_stop(model):
56	    """Check that we respect the stop words."""
57	
58	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(temperature=0))
59	    split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
60	
61	    STOP_IDX = 5
62	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop=split_text[STOP_IDX])
63	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
64	    new_split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
65	
66	    # Output should not contain the stop word.
67	    assert len(new_split_text) == STOP_IDX
68	
69	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0,
70	                            stop=split_text[STOP_IDX],
71	                            include_stop_str_in_output=True)
72	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
73	    new_split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
74	
75	    # Output should contain the stop word.
76	    assert len(new_split_text) == STOP_IDX + 1
77	
78	
79	def test_stop_token_ids(model):
80	    """Check that we respect the stop token ids."""
81	
82	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(temperature=0))
83	
84	    stop_token_id_0 = output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[5]
85	    stop_token_id_1 = output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[6]
86	
87	    stop_token_ids = [stop_token_id_1, stop_token_id_0]
88	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids)
89	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
90	    assert output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[-1] == stop_token_id_0
91	
92	    stop_token_ids = [stop_token_id_0, stop_token_id_1]
93	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids)
94	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
95	    assert output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[-1] == stop_token_id_0
96	
97	
98	def test_detokenize_false(model):
99	    """Check that detokenize=False option works."""
100	
101	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(detokenize=False))
102	    assert len(output[0].outputs[0].token_ids) > 0
103	    assert len(output[0].outputs[0].text) == 0
104	
105	    output = model.generate(
106	        PROMPT, SamplingParams(detokenize=False, logprobs=3,
107	                               prompt_logprobs=3))
108	    assert len(output[0].outputs[0].token_ids) > 0
109	    assert len(output[0].outputs[0].text) == 0
110	
111	    prompt_logprobs = output[0].prompt_logprobs
112	    sampled_logprobs = output[0].outputs[0].logprobs
113	    assert len(prompt_logprobs) > 1
114	    assert len(sampled_logprobs) > 1
115	    for all_logprobs in (prompt_logprobs[1:], sampled_logprobs):
116	        for logprobs in all_logprobs:
117	            assert 3 <= len(logprobs) <= 4
118	            assert all(lp.decoded_token is None for lp in logprobs.values())
119	
120	
121	def test_bad_words(model):
122	    """Check that we respect bad words."""
123	
124	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(temperature=0))
125	    split_text = output[0].outputs[0].text.split()
126	
127	    bad_words_1 = " ".join(split_text[:2])
128	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0, bad_words=[bad_words_1])
129	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
130	    new_text = output[0].outputs[0].text
131	    assert bad_words_1 not in new_text
132	
133	    bad_words_2 = new_text.split()[-1]
134	    params = SamplingParams(temperature=0,
135	                            bad_words=[bad_words_1, bad_words_2])
136	    output = model.generate(PROMPT, params)
137	    new_text = output[0].outputs[0].text
138	    assert bad_words_1 not in new_text
139	    assert bad_words_2 not in new_text
140	
141	
142	def test_logits_processor(model):
143	    """Check that we reject logits processor."""
144	
145	    # This sample logits processor gives infinite score to the i-th token,
146	    # where i is the length of the input sequence.
147	    # We therefore expect the output token sequence to be [0, 1, 2, ...]
148	    def pick_ith(token_ids, logits):
149	        logits[len(token_ids)] = float("inf")
150	        return logits
151	
152	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
153	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT,
154	                           SamplingParams(logits_processors=[pick_ith]))
155	
156	
157	def test_allowed_token_ids(model):
158	    """Check that we can use allowed_token_ids."""
159	
160	    TOKEN_ID = 10
161	    allowed_token_ids = [TOKEN_ID]
162	    output = model.generate(
163	        PROMPT, SamplingParams(allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids))
164	    assert output[0].outputs[0].token_ids[-1] == TOKEN_ID
165	
166	    # Reject empty allowed_token_ids.
167	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
168	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(allowed_token_ids=[]))
169	
170	    # Reject negative token id.
171	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
172	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(allowed_token_ids=[-1]))
173	
174	    # Reject out of vocabulary.
175	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
176	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT,
177	                           SamplingParams(allowed_token_ids=[10000000]))
178	
179	
180	def test_priority(model):
181	    """Check that we reject requests with priority."""
182	
183	    # Reject all allowed token ids
184	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
185	        _ = model.generate(PROMPT, priority=[1])
186	
187	
188	def test_seed(model):
189	    """Check that seed impacts randomness."""
190	
191	    out_1 = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(seed=42))
192	    out_2 = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(seed=42))
193	    out_3 = model.generate(PROMPT, SamplingParams(seed=43))
194	
195	    assert out_1[0].outputs[0].text == out_2[0].outputs[0].text
196	    assert out_1[0].outputs[0].text != out_3[0].outputs[0].text
197

22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)

121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native

69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)

85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)

109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)

121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234

236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

545	    def _get_guided_json_from_tool(
546	            self) -> Optional[Union[str, dict, BaseModel]]:
547	        # user has chosen to not use any tool
548	        if self.tool_choice == "none" or self.tools is None:
549	            return None
550	
551	        # user has chosen to use a named tool
552	        if type(self.tool_choice) is ChatCompletionNamedToolChoiceParam:
553	            tool_name = self.tool_choice.function.name
554	            tools = {tool.function.name: tool.function for tool in self.tools}
555	            if tool_name not in tools:
556	                raise ValueError(
557	                    f"Tool '{tool_name}' has not been passed in `tools`.")
558	            tool = tools[tool_name]
559	            return tool.parameters
560	
561	        if self.tool_choice == "required":
562	            # Pydantic schema generation cannot be used since the JSON schema
563	            # has to be constructed for a specific instantiation of a tool list
564	            # so that parameters of a function are correctly generated
565	            # based on the chosen function name
566	            def get_tool_schema(tool: ChatCompletionToolsParam) -> dict:
567	                return {
568	                    "properties": {
569	                        "name": {
570	                            "type": "string",
571	                            "enum": [tool.function.name]
572	                        },
573	                        # parameters are always generated as '{}' in the final
574	                        # output if they are missing from the request
575	                        # (i.e. are None or '{}') so the schema is
576	                        # updated to produce an empty object in that case
577	                        "parameters": tool.function.parameters
578	                        if tool.function.parameters else {
579	                            "type": "object",
580	                            "properties": {}
581	                        }
582	                    },
583	                    "required": ["name", "parameters"]
584	                }
585	
586	            json_schema = {
587	                "type": "array",
588	                "minItems": 1,
589	                "items": {
590	                    "type": "object",
591	                    "anyOf": [get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in self.tools]
592	                }
593	            }
594	            return json_schema
595	
596	        return None

600	    def validate_stream_options(cls, data):
601	        if data.get("stream_options") and not data.get("stream"):
602	            raise ValueError(
603	                "Stream options can only be defined when `stream=True`.")
604	
605	        return data

607	    @model_validator(mode="before")
608	    @classmethod
609	    def check_logprobs(cls, data):
610	        if (prompt_logprobs := data.get("prompt_logprobs")) is not None:
611	            if data.get("stream") and prompt_logprobs > 0:
612	                raise ValueError(
613	                    "`prompt_logprobs` are not available when `stream=True`.")
614	
615	            if prompt_logprobs < 0:

168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits

236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()

23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling

117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.

124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,

128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled

131	        sampled = torch.where(

133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled

54	def _create_allowed_token_ids(
55	    batch_size: int,
56	    vocab_size: int,
57	    num_allowed_token_ids: int,
58	    device: torch.device,
59	) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
60	    mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
61	    for i in range(batch_size):
62	        if i % 2 == 1:
63	            continue
64	        if mask is None:
65	            mask = torch.zeros((batch_size, vocab_size),
66	                               dtype=torch.bool,
67	                               device=device)
68	        start = min(i, vocab_size - 1)
69	        end = min(i + num_allowed_token_ids, vocab_size - 1)
70	        mask[i, start:end] = True
71	    return mask

77	    bad_words_token_ids = {}

83	                                         replace=True).tolist()
84	            token_ids_single_batch.append(token_ids)
85	        bad_words_token_ids[batch_idx] = token_ids_single_batch
86	    if batch_size >= 2:
87	        # Test no bad_words for some batch
88	        no_bad_words_batch_idx = np.random.choice(batch_size)
89	        bad_words_token_ids.pop(no_bad_words_batch_idx, None)
90	    return bad_words_token_ids

93	def _update_output_token_ids_for_bad_words(
94	        metadata: SamplingMetadata, vocab_size: int) -> dict[int, list[int]]:
95	    bad_words_last_tokens = {}
96	    for batch_idx, bad_words_token_ids in metadata.bad_words_token_ids.items():
97	        output_token_ids = metadata.output_token_ids[batch_idx]
98	        bad_words_last_token: list[int] = []
99	        for i, bad_word_token_ids in enumerate(bad_words_token_ids):
100	            if len(bad_word_token_ids) == 1:
101	                # Single token id always affects logits
102	                bad_words_last_token.append(bad_word_token_ids[0])
103	            else:
104	                prefix_length = len(bad_word_token_ids) - 1
105	                has_bad_words = np.random.choice([True, False])
106	                if has_bad_words:
107	                    output_token_ids[-prefix_length:] = bad_word_token_ids[:-1]
108	                    bad_words_last_token.append(bad_word_token_ids[-1])
109	                    break  # Maximum one update to output_token_ids
110	                else:  # Make sure no accidental match to bad words
111	                    output_token_ids[-1] = (bad_word_token_ids[-2] +
112	                                            1) % vocab_size
113	        bad_words_last_tokens[batch_idx] = bad_words_last_token
114	    return bad_words_last_tokens

4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	import torch
7	import torch.nn as nn
8	
9	from vllm import envs
10	from vllm.logger import init_logger
11	from vllm.platforms import current_platform
12	
13	logger = init_logger(__name__)
14	
15	try:
16	    import flashinfer.sampling
17	    is_flashinfer_available = True
18	except ImportError:
19	    is_flashinfer_available = False
20	
21	
22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed report for the user.

## vLLM Logits/Probability Filtering Implementation Report

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and found the complete data flow for logits filtering. Here's the comprehensive breakdown:

### 1. SAMPLING PARAMETERS (Entry Point)

**File: `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (Lines 118-603)**

The `SamplingParams` class defines the sampling configuration:
- `top_p` (line 212): Float ∈ (0, 1] - cumulative probability threshold
- `top_k` (line 213): Int ≥ 0 - number of top tokens to consider
- `min_p` (line 214): Float ∈ [0, 1] - minimum relative probability filter

Validation occurs in `_verify_args()` (lines 387-462), confirming parameter constraints.

---

### 2. METADATA STRUCTURES (Data Carriers)

#### v0 (Legacy) Architecture:
**File: `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (Lines 1-598)**

**`SequenceGroupToSample` dataclass (lines 20-62):**
- Contains `sampling_params: SamplingParams` that carries top_p, top_k, min_p

**`SamplingTensors` dataclass (lines 372-597):**
- Created by `from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 386-495)
- Batches sampling parameters into GPU tensors:
  - `top_ps: torch.Tensor` (line 377)
  - `top_ks: torch.Tensor` (line 378)
  - `min_ps: torch.Tensor` (line 379)
- Lines 426-430: Flags indicate whether to apply each filter:
  ```python
  do_top_p_top_k = (top_p < 1.0 or top_k != vocab_size)
  do_min_p = (min_p > eps)
  ```

#### v1 (New) Architecture:
**File: `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` (Lines 1-44)**

**`SamplingMetadata` dataclass:**
- `top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]` (line 19)
- `top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]` (line 20)
- `logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager` (line 43) - handles min_p via `MinPLogitsProcessor`

---

### 3. FILTERING IMPLEMENTATIONS

#### V0: Direct Logits Filtering

**File: `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`**

**`_apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 398-424):**
```
1. Sort logits in ascending order (line 403)
2. Apply top_k mask (lines 406-410):
   - Find k-th highest logit value
   - Set all logits < k-th value to -inf
3. Apply top_p mask (lines 413-418):
   - Convert sorted logits to probabilities
   - Cumulative sum of probabilities
   - Mark tokens where cumsum <= (1 - p) as -inf
   - Always keep at least 1 token (line 417)
4. Scatter logits back to original order (lines 421-423)
```

**`_apply_min_p()` (lines 427-441):**
```
1. Softmax logits to get probabilities (line 435)
2. Find max probability per sequence (line 436)
3. Calculate threshold = min_p * max_prob (line 437)
4. Mask tokens where prob < threshold to -inf (line 439)
```

These filters are called in Sampler.forward() (lines 284-289):
```python
if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
    logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                sampling_tensors.top_ks)
if do_min_p:
    logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
```

---

#### V1: Modular Logits Processing Pipeline

**File: `/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` (Lines 225-307)**

**`MinPLogitsProcessor` class:**
```python
def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    probability_values = softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    max_probabilities = amax(probability_values, dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities * min_p  # line 301
    invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p  # line 303
    logits[invalid_token_mask] = -inf  # line 305
    return logits
```

**File: `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`**

**`apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 168-208):**
```
Same algorithm as v0 but with optimized tensor operations
```

**`apply_top_k_only()` (lines 211-233):**
Optimized variant avoiding full sorting for top_k-only case

---

### 4. COMPLETE DATA FLOW

```
SamplingParams (sampling_params.py)
  ↓
  top_p, top_k, min_p validated in __post_init__()
  ↓
V0: SequenceGroupToSample.sampling_params
    ↓
    SamplingMetadata.prepare() extracts from seq_groups
    ↓
    SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata() (line 387)
      ├→ Lines 415-416: Extract top_p, min_p from sampling_params
      ├→ Lines 426-430: Determine do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p flags
      └→ Returns batched tensors for GPU
    ↓
    Sampler.forward() (line 266-289)
      ├→ _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps, top_ks)
      └→ _apply_min_p(logits, min_ps)
    ↓
    Result: Filtered logits → Multinomial Sampling

V1: SamplingMetadata (metadata.py)
    ↓
    top_p, top_k as torch.Tensor
    ↓
    Sampler.forward() (line 90-137)
      ├→ TopKTopPSampler() (line 121)
      │   ├→ apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
      │   └→ random_sample(probs)
      │
      └→ MinPLogitsProcessor.apply() (via logitsprocs)
    ↓
    Result: Sampled tokens
```

---

### 5. LOGITS PROCESSOR LAYER

**File: `/vllm/model_executor/layers/logits_processor.py` (Lines 1-198)**

The `LogitsProcessor` layer applies custom logits processors from `SamplingParams.logits_processors`:
- Lines 82-84: Calls `_apply_logits_processors(logits, sampling_metadata)`
- Lines 149-173: Applies per-sequence logits processors
- Used for guided decoding, bad word filtering, etc.

---

### 6. KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS FOR "TOP_A" ADDITION

For implementing a "top_a" sampler (keeping tokens with prob >= top_a * max_prob):

**Pattern to follow (from min_p implementation):**

1. **Add to SamplingParams** (`sampling_params.py`):
   - Add field: `top_a: float = 0.0` (default disabled)
   - Add validation: `0.0 <= top_a <= 1.0`

2. **V0 Implementation** (sampler.py):
   ```python
   def _apply_top_a(logits: torch.Tensor, top_a: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
       probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
       top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
       scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs  # Same as min_p
       tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
       logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
       return logits
   ```

3. **SamplingTensors** (sampling_metadata.py):
   - Add `top_as: torch.Tensor` field (line 379)
   - Extract in `from_sampling_metadata()` (line 399)
   - Add flag `do_top_a` check (line 429)

4. **V1 Implementation** (v1/sample/logits_processor.py):
   - Create `TopALogitsProcessor` class following `MinPLogitsProcessor` pattern (lines 225-307)
   - Register in `init_builtin_logitsprocs()` (lines 488-517)

5. **Metadata** (v1/sample/metadata.py):
   - Add `top_a: Optional[torch.Tensor]` field (line 20)

---

### 7. CRITICAL CODE LOCATIONS FOR YOUR IMPLEMENTATION

| Component | File | Lines | Purpose |
|-----------|------|-------|---------|
| Parameter Definition | `/vllm/sampling_params.py` | 118-603 | Define top_a field and validation |
| V0 Filtering Logic | `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 398-441 | Add _apply_top_a() function |
| V0 Sampling Tensors | `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 372-495 | Add top_a tensor batching |
| V1 Filtering Logic | `/vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | 225-307 | Add TopALogitsProcessor class |
| V1 Metadata | `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | 1-44 | Add top_a field to dataclass |
| V1 Sampler | `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | 1-227 | Integrate TopALogitsProcessor |

The implementation is straightforward because **top_a uses the exact same logic as min_p** - both compute: `threshold = param * max_probability` and filter tokens below it.

3	from typing import Final
4	
5	import pytest
6	import schemathesis
7	from hypothesis import settings
8	from schemathesis import GenerationConfig
9	
10	from ...utils import RemoteOpenAIServer
11	
12	schemathesis.experimental.OPEN_API_3_1.enable()
13	
14	MODEL_NAME = "HuggingFaceTB/SmolVLM-256M-Instruct"
15	MAXIMUM_IMAGES = 2
16	DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: Final[int] = 10
17	LONG_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: Final[int] = 60
18	
19	
20	@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
21	def server():
22	    args = [
23	        "--task",
24	        "generate",
25	        "--max-model-len",
26	        "2048",
27	        "--max-num-seqs",
28	        "5",
29	        "--enforce-eager",
30	        "--trust-remote-code",
31	        "--limit-mm-per-prompt",
32	        f"image={MAXIMUM_IMAGES}",
33	    ]
34	
35	    with RemoteOpenAIServer(MODEL_NAME, args) as remote_server:
36	        yield remote_server
37	
38	
39	@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
40	def get_schema(server):
41	    # avoid generating null (\x00) bytes in strings during test case generation
42	    return schemathesis.openapi.from_uri(
43	        f"{server.url_root}/openapi.json",
44	        generation_config=GenerationConfig(allow_x00=False),
45	    )
46	
47	
48	schema = schemathesis.from_pytest_fixture("get_schema")
49	
50	
51	@schemathesis.hook
52	def before_generate_case(context: schemathesis.hooks.HookContext, strategy):
53	    op = context.operation
54	    assert op is not None
55	
56	    def no_file_type(case: schemathesis.models.Case):
57	        """
58	        This filter skips test cases for the `POST /tokenize` endpoint where the
59	        HTTP request body uses `"type": "file"` in any message's content.
60	        We expect these cases to fail because that type isn't implemented here
61	        https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/0b34593017953051b3225b1483ce0f4670e3eb0e/vllm/entrypoints/chat_utils.py#L1038-L1095
62	
63	        Example test cases that are skipped:
64	        curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
65	            -d '{"messages": [{"role": "assistant"}, {"content": [{"file": {}, "type": "file"}], "role": "user"}]}' \
66	            http://localhost:8000/tokenize
67	
68	        curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
69	            -d '{"messages": [{"content": [{"file": {}, "type": "file"}], "role": "user"}]}' \
70	            http://localhost:8000/tokenize
71	        """  # noqa: E501
72	        if (op.method.lower() == "post" and op.path == "/tokenize"
73	                and hasattr(case, "body") and isinstance(case.body, dict)
74	                and "messages" in case.body
75	                and isinstance(case.body["messages"], list)
76	                and len(case.body["messages"]) > 0):
77	            for message in case.body["messages"]:
78	                if not isinstance(message, dict):
79	                    continue
80	                content = message.get("content", [])
81	                if not isinstance(content, list) or len(content) == 0:
82	                    continue
83	                if any(item.get("type") == "file" for item in content):
84	                    return False
85	        return True
86	
87	    return strategy.filter(no_file_type)
88	
89

91	@schema.override(headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"})
92	@settings(deadline=LONG_TIMEOUT_SECONDS * 1000)
93	def test_openapi_stateless(case: schemathesis.Case):
94	    key = (

96	        case.operation.path,
97	    )
98	    timeout = {
99	        # requires a longer timeout
100	        ("POST", "/v1/chat/completions"):
101	        LONG_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
102	    }.get(key, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
103	
104	    #No need to verify SSL certificate for localhost
105	    case.call_and_validate(verify=False, timeout=timeout)

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary report with all the key findings:

## Summary Report: vLLM Sampling Parameter Structure

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase to understand how sampling parameters are defined, validated, and used. Here are the comprehensive findings:

### 1. **SamplingParams Class Definition**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496099044-edact3/vllm/sampling_params.py`

**Key sampling filter fields (Lines 205-245):**

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|-------|------|---------|-------|
| `temperature` | float | 1.0 | Controls randomness; <1e-5 triggers greedy sampling |
| `top_p` | float | 1.0 | Cumulative probability threshold; must be in (0, 1] |
| `top_k` | int | 0 | Number of top tokens; can be 0/-1 (disabled) or ≥1 |
| `min_p` | float | 0.0 | Minimum probability threshold; must be in [0, 1] |
| `presence_penalty` | float | 0.0 | Penalize by token presence; range [-2, 2] |
| `frequency_penalty` | float | 0.0 | Penalize by token frequency; range [-2, 2] |
| `repetition_penalty` | float | 1.0 | Penalize repetition; must be > 0.0 |

**Validation Logic (Lines 387-462 in `_verify_args()` and `_verify_greedy_sampling()`):**
- **top_p validation (Line 417-418):** `if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0: raise ValueError`
- **top_k validation (Lines 420-425):** `if self.top_k < -1` or not isinstance top_k int
- **min_p validation (Lines 426-428):** `if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0`
- **Special behavior (Lines 377-382):** When temperature < 1e-5, forces greedy sampling by setting: `top_p=1.0, top_k=0, min_p=0.0`

### 2. **SamplingTensors Class - Tensor Conversion**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775496099044-edact3/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (Lines 372-597)

**SamplingTensors dataclass fields (Lines 376-384):**
```python
temperatures: torch.Tensor
top_ps: torch.Tensor
top_ks: torch.Tensor
min_ps: torch.Tensor
presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Conversion process in `from_sampling_metadata()` (Lines 387-495):**
- Collects per-sequence parameters from SamplingParams into lists (Lines 394-402)
- Determines flags for which filters to apply: `do_penalties`, `do_top_p_top_k`, `do_min_p` (Lines 403-434)
- Converts lists to GPU tensors with pinned memory (Lines 481-597)
- Top-k is clamped to vocab size (Line 419-420): `top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)`

### 3. **Sampler Implementation - V0 (Standard)**

**Key sampling filter fields (Lines 205-245):**

| Field | Type | Default | Notes |
|-------|------|---------|-------|
| `temperature` | float | 1.0 | Controls randomness; <1e-5 triggers greedy sampling |
| `top_p` | float | 1.0 | Cumulative probability threshold; must be in (0, 1] |
| `top_k` | int | 0 | Number of top tokens; can be 0/-1 (disabled) or ≥1 |
| `min_p` | float | 0.0 | Minimum probability threshold; must be in [0, 1] |
| `presence_penalty` | float | 0.0 | Penalize by token presence; range [-2, 2] |
| `frequency_penalty` | float | 0.0 | Penalize by token frequency; range [-2, 2] |
| `repetition_penalty` | float | 1.0 | Penalize repetition; must be > 0.0 |

**Validation Logic (Lines 387-462 in `_verify_args()` and `_verify_greedy_sampling()`):**

- **top_k validation (Lines 420-425):** `if self.top_k < -1` or not isinstance top_k int
- **min_p validation (Lines 426-428):** `if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0`
- **Special behavior (Lines 377-382):** When temperature < 1e-5, forces greedy sampling by setting: `top_p=1.0, top_k=0, min_p=0.0`

38	class BatchUpdate:
39	    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
40	    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch
41	
42	    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
43	    # within the persistent batch.
44	    #
45	    # Note: each added request is represented as
46	    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
47	    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the

49	    # the logits processors always see the latest list of

51	    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
52	    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]

56	class BatchUpdateBuilder:
57	    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
58	    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
59	    
60	    Assumptions:
61	    * All information about requests removed from persistent batch
62	      during a step is aggregated in self._removed through calls to
63	      self.removed_append() at the beginning of a step. This must happen
64	      before the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
65	      or self.peek_removed() are invoked in a given step
66	    * After the first time that self.removed, self.pop_removed()
67	      or self.peek_removed() are read in a step, no new removals
68	      are registered using self.removed_append()
69	    * Elements of self._removed are never directly modified, added or
70	      removed (i.e. modification is only via self.removed_append() and
71	      self.pop_removed())
72	    
73	    Guarantees under above assumptions:
74	    * self.removed is always sorted in descending order
75	    * self.pop_removed() and self.peek_removed() both return
76	      the lowest removed request index in the current step
77	    """
78	
79	    _removed: list[RemovedRequest]
80	    _is_removed_sorted: bool
81	    moved: list[MovedRequest]
82	    added: list[AddedRequest]
83	
84	    def __init__(
85	        self,
86	        removed: Optional[list[RemovedRequest]] = None,
87	        moved: Optional[list[MovedRequest]] = None,
88	        added: Optional[list[AddedRequest]] = None,
89	    ) -> None:
90	        self._removed = removed or []
91	        self.moved = moved or []
92	        self.added = added or []
93	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
94	
95	    def _ensure_removed_sorted(self) -> None:
96	        """Sort removed request indices in
97	        descending order.
98	        
99	        Idempotent after first call in a
100	        given step, until reset.
101	        """
102	        if not self._is_removed_sorted:
103	            self._removed.sort(reverse=True)
104	            self._is_removed_sorted = True
105	
106	    @property
107	    def removed(self) -> list[RemovedRequest]:
108	        """Removed request indices sorted in
109	        descending order"""
110	        self._ensure_removed_sorted()
111	        return self._removed
112	
113	    def removed_append(self, index: int) -> None:
114	        """Register the removal of a request from
115	        the persistent batch.
116	
117	        Must not be called after the first time
118	        self.removed, self.pop_removed() or
119	        self.peek_removed() are invoked.
120	        
121	        Args:
122	          index: request index
123	        """
124	        if self._is_removed_sorted:
125	            raise RuntimeError("Cannot register new removed request after"
126	                               " self.removed has been read.")
127	        self._removed.append(index)
128	
129	    def has_removed(self) -> bool:
130	        return bool(self._removed)
131	
132	    def peek_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
133	        """Return lowest removed request index"""
134	        if self.has_removed():
135	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
136	            return self._removed[-1]
137	        return None
138	
139	    def pop_removed(self) -> Optional[int]:
140	        """Pop lowest removed request index"""
141	        if self.has_removed():
142	            self._ensure_removed_sorted()
143	            return self._removed.pop()
144	        return None
145	
146	    def get_and_reset(self, batch_size: int) -> Optional[BatchUpdate]:
147	        """Generate a logitsprocs batch update data structure
148	        and reset internal batch update builder state.
149	        
150	        Args:
151	          batch_size: current persistent batch size
152	
153	        Returns:
154	          Frozen logitsprocs batch update instance; `None` if no updates
155	        """
156	        # Reset removal-sorting logic
157	        self._is_removed_sorted = False
158	        if not any((self._removed, self.moved, self.added)):
159	            # No update; short-circuit
160	            return None
161	        # Build batch state update
162	        batch_update = BatchUpdate(
163	            batch_size=batch_size,
164	            removed=self._removed,
165	            moved=self.moved,
166	            added=self.added,
167	        )
168	        # Reset removed/moved/added update lists
169	        self._removed = []
170	        self.moved = []
171	        self.added = []
172	        return batch_update

225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

3	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
4	import copy
5	from dataclasses import dataclass
6	from enum import Enum, IntEnum

21	_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2
